prompts: An ecological pyramid illustrates the trophic levels in an ecosystem. It starts with producers at the base, who harness solar energy to produce food through photosynthesis. Above them are primary consumers, usually herbivores, followed by secondary consumers, typically carnivores or omnivores. Tertiary consumers, which are often top predators, occupy the next level. Energy flow decreases as it moves up the pyramid, with only about 10% of the energy transferred to the next level, due to energy loss in metabolic processes. This structure underscores the efficiency of energy transfer and the reliance of higher trophic levels on the energy captured by producers.
